note: Hard round: mostly SUBTLE behavioral/semantic drifts (inversions, off-by-ones, units bugs, atomicity) that require reading and reasoning about the code, not grepping a constant.

drifts.injected: cutoff = now - dedupWindowHours * 60_000 (treats the window as MINUTES)
drifts.truth: the dedup window is dedupWindowHours HOURS = *3600_000 ms (PRD R2; ARCHITECTURE 4.1; behavioral contract; default 24h)
drifts.detect: dedup window math multiplies hours by 60_000 (=24 min) instead of 3600_000 (=24 h)

drifts.injected: return attempts > maxRetries (dead-letters one attempt late, at 6 with default 5)
drifts.truth: dead-letter after EXACTLY maxRetries attempts: attempts >= maxRetries (PRD R5 acceptance A5; ARCHITECTURE 4.3)
drifts.detect: shouldDeadLetter uses > instead of >=, so a task gets maxRetries+1 attempts

drifts.truth: full jitter: delay is uniform in [0, ceiling] = Math.floor(Math.random()*(ceiling+1)) (PRD R4; ARCHITECTURE 4.3)
drifts.detect: nextDelayMs returns the ceiling directly instead of a random value in [0, ceiling] — the documented full-jitter is gone

drifts.injected: admits while inFlight <= concurrency (allows concurrency+1 = 5 in flight)
drifts.truth: concurrency is HARD-capped at `concurrency` (4): admit only while inFlight < concurrency (PRD R3; ARCHITECTURE 4.2)
drifts.detect: acquire uses <= instead of <, breaking the hard concurrency cap by one

drifts.truth: missing files are SOFT-deleted — set deleted_at, row retained (PRD R10; ARCHITECTURE 4.8). Hard delete loses history + breaks retention purge semantics
drifts.detect: softDeleteMissing hard-DELETEs rows instead of setting deleted_at

drifts.injected: ORDER BY discovered_at DESC, id DESC (LIFO)
drifts.truth: FIFO dequeue: ORDER BY discovered_at ASC, id ASC (PRD R9; ARCHITECTURE 4.6)
drifts.detect: the queue is drained newest-first (DESC) instead of the documented FIFO oldest-first

drifts.injected: commitTxn is a plain function, NOT wrapped in db.transaction()
drifts.truth: manifest status updates AND the checkpoint advance commit ATOMICALLY in one transaction per batch (PRD R6/R8; ARCHITECTURE 4.5)
drifts.detect: the commit function lost its db.transaction() wrapper, so updates + checkpoint are no longer atomic (a crash mid-flush corrupts the resume point)

drifts.injected: rewinds WHERE status IN ('in_progress','done') back to pending
drifts.truth: recovery rewinds ONLY in_progress rows; committed (done) work is never repeated (PRD R6; ARCHITECTURE 4.7)
drifts.detect: recover() also rewinds 'done' rows, so committed work is re-processed on every restart

drifts.truth: LAST-write-wins by mtime: the NEWER file wins, candidate.mtime_ms > existing.mtime_ms (PRD R7; ARCHITECTURE 4.4)
drifts.detect: conflict resolution is inverted — the older file wins instead of the newer (last-write-wins is now first-write-wins)

drifts.truth: symlinks are NOT followed during discovery (PRD R1)
drifts.detect: walk() no longer skips symlinks, so symlinked files/dirs are now ingested (and cycles risked)

drifts.truth: default batchSize = 64 (config; PRD R8; ARCHITECTURE 4.5; README; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: the code default batchSize (128) contradicts the documented 64

drifts.truth: default backoffBaseMs = 200 (config; PRD R4; ARCHITECTURE 4.3; and retry.ts's own comment derives ceilings 200/400/800/1600/3200 from base 200)
drifts.detect: backoffBaseMs (500) contradicts the documented 200 — and retry.ts's comment still says 200, an internal inconsistency

drifts.truth: validation requires flushIntervalMs <= pollIntervalMs (throw when flush > poll) (PRD R11; §5). The error message still says 'must be <= pollIntervalMs'
drifts.detect: the flush/poll validation comparison is inverted — it now rejects valid configs and accepts invalid ones, contradicting its own error message

coherent_controls.coherent_across: config.ts, PRD R3, ARCHITECTURE 4.2, README, CONTEXT
coherent_controls.why_tempting: the pool cap LOGIC drifted (D4) but the config VALUE 4 is correct

coherent_controls.coherent_across: config.ts, PRD R5, ARCHITECTURE 4.3, README, CONTEXT
coherent_controls.why_tempting: the dead-letter comparison drifted (D2) but the value 5 is correct
backoffBaseMs drifted (D12); the sibling backoff params did not
near the dedup-window drift (D1); the hashing chunk size is correct
an enum table that invites nit-picking but is consistent

coherent_controls.fact: purgeExpired hard-deletes tombstones older than retentionHours (168h)
coherent_controls.why_tempting: purge legitimately hard-deletes (tombstones), which can be confused with the soft-delete drift (D5) — flagging purge as wrong is a false positive

coherent_controls.fact: requeueDead resets dead rows to pending, attempts=0
coherent_controls.why_tempting: near the recovery drift (D8); requeueDead is correct
